author: Chris@ZooClaw
message:
test(web): regression contract for read-excel-file/browser default export (#1546)

## Why
Dependabot is bumping `read-excel-file` 8.0.3 → 9.0.0 in #1541. The only
consumer in the repo is
`web/src/components/artifacts/renderers/ExcelRenderer.tsx`, which is
**excluded from coverage** (`src/components/artifacts/renderers/**` in
`vitest.config.mts`) — so a breaking change in the parser would only
surface in production.

This PR adds a contract test that locks the surface ExcelRenderer
actually reads, then we verify it passes against both versions before
greenlighting the bump.

## What's locked
- Default export shape: `Promise<{sheet: string; data: Cell[][]}[]>`
- Multi-sheet workbook traversal (we read all sheets, not just the
first)
- Cell-type round-trip:
  - `string` → `string`
  - `number` (incl. signed) → `number`
  - `boolean` → `boolean`
  - Excel date → JS `Date` instance with correct ISO timestamp
- empty cell → `null` (ExcelRenderer's `formatCell` branches on `===
null`)
- UTF-8 round-trip (cyrillic in the fixture)

## Verification
Ran `pnpm exec vitest run tests/unit/lib/read-excel-file.unit.spec.ts`
against both versions:

| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 8.0.3 (current lockfile) | ✅ 3/3 passed |
| 9.0.0 (Dependabot target) | ✅ 3/3 passed |

So the bump is safe per the consumer's actual surface. The 9.0.0
changelog confirms this — only `parseData()` was reworked, and we don't
use it.

## Fixture
`web/tests/unit/fixtures/read-excel-file.fixture.xlsx` (5.5KB) —
generated once via openpyxl. Two sheets ("Mixed" with mixed cell types +
1 empty cell + cyrillic; "Empty" with header only) to exercise both the
populated and the trivial cases.

## Test plan
- [ ] CI green
- [ ] After merge, #1541 should still be green (it doesn't depend on
this; this just buys runtime safety net for any future major bump on
this lib)

author: Chris@ZooClaw
message:
chore(lint): ban TypeScript `enum` via no-restricted-syntax (#1545)

## Summary
- Adds an ESLint `no-restricted-syntax` rule (`TSEnumDeclaration`
selector) banning `enum` / `const enum` in `web/` — covers both `src/**`
(Block 5) and `tests/unit/**` (Block 8) since flat-config overrides are
full-replace, not array-merged.
- Codebase has **zero enums today** (verified by grep across 1,074
TS/TSX files in `web/`), so this is a regression guard — no migration,
no shrink-only ignores list, no CI script needed.
- Documents the rationale + idiomatic alternatives in a new `##
TypeScript Conventions` section of `web/AGENTS.md` (auto-syncs to
`CLAUDE.md` via the existing symlink).

## Why ban enum
- Compiles to a runtime IIFE that doesn't tree-shake reliably (violates
TS's "types disappear at runtime" philosophy)
- Numeric variants are not type-safe — `enum S { A } ; handle(999)`
compiles
- `const enum` behaves inconsistently under `--isolatedModules` (Vite /
esbuild / SWC default)
- String values traveling on the wire are clearer with string literal
unions; matches the backend `services/claw-interface/` Pydantic
`StrEnum` contract zero-friction

## Replacements (already the project pattern)
- Type-only constraint → string literal union: `type PlanTier =
'starter' | 'pro' | 'ultra'` (see `src/lib/stripe/stripe.ts`)
- Needs iteration / reverse lookup / metadata → `as const` map: `const
PLAN_INFO = { starter: {...}, pro: {...} } as const; type PlanTier =
keyof typeof PLAN_INFO` (see `src/components/billing/constants.ts`)

## Test plan
- [ ] CI `code-quality / lint-and-test` green
- [ ] Local sanity check: temporarily add `export enum Foo { A, B }` to
any `src/**/*.ts`, run `pnpm --filter web lint`, confirm the new error
fires (message starts with "禁止使用 TypeScript \`enum\`"), then delete the
file
- [ ] Same check inside `tests/unit/**` to confirm Block 8 mirror works
- [ ] `pnpm --filter web lint` with no temp file stays at the existing
baseline (no new errors)

author: dependabot[bot]
message:
chore(deps): bump read-excel-file from 8.0.3 to 9.0.1 in /web (#1541) Bumps [read-excel-file](https://gitlab.com/catamphetamine/read-excel-file) from 8.0.3 to 9.0.1.
Changelog

Sourced from read-excel-file's changelog.

9.0.7 / 28.04.2026

  • Merged Toni G's fix for a bug when error.row was always equal to 1.
  • Re-added schema parameter in readSheet() function.

9.0.0 / 18.04.2026

  • If you were using parseData() function:
    • Rewrote the code of the parseData() function and renamed it to parseSheetData().
    • The result of parseSheetData() function is now { errors, objects }. If there're no errors, errors will be undefined. Otherwise, errors will be a non-empty array and objects will be undefined.
      • Previously the result of parseSheetData() function was [{ errors, object }, ...], i.e. the errors were split between each particular data row. Now the errors are combined for all data rows. The rationale is that it's simpler to handle the result of the function this way.
      • Re-added row: number property to the error object. It's the number of the data row that caused the error, starting from 1.
      • Added columnIndex: number property to the error object.
    • Renamed some of the exported TypeScript types:
      • ParseDataCustomType → ParseSheetDataCustomType
      • ParseDataCustomTypeErrorMessage → ParseSheetDataCustomTypeErrorMessage
      • ParseDataCustomTypeErrorReason → ParseSheetDataCustomTypeErrorReason
      • ParseDataError → ParseSheetDataError
      • ParseDataValueRequiredError → ParseSheetDataValueRequiredError
      • ParseDataResult → ParseSheetDataResult
    • In a schema, a nested object could be declared as: { required: true/false, schema: { ... } }. This is still true but the required flag is now only allowed to be either undefined or false, so true value is not allowed. The reason is quite simple. If a nested object as a whole is marked as required: true, and then it happens to be empty, a "required" error should be returned for it. But that error would also have to include a column title, and a nested object simply can't be pinned down to a single column in a sheet because it is by definition spread over multiple columns. So instead of marking a nested object as a whole with required: true, mark the specific required properties of it.

8.0.0 / 11.03.2026

  • If you were using the default exported function:

    • Renamed the default exported function to a named exported function readSheet.
      • Old: import readExcelFile from "read-excel-file/browser"
      • New: import { readSheet } from "read-excel-file/browser"
      • And same for other exports like "read-excel-file/node", etc.
    • The default exported function now returns a different kind of result. Specifically, now it returns all available sheets — an array of objects: [{ sheet: "Sheet 1", data: [['a1','b1','c1'],['a2','b2','c2']] }, ...].
    • The default exported function used to return sheet names when passed getSheets: true parameter. Now, instead of that, the default exported function just returns all available sheets, from which one could get the sheet names.

  • If you were using readSheetNames() function:

    • Removed exported function readSheetNames(). Use the default exported function instead. The default exported function now returns all sheets.

  • If you were using parseExcelDate() function:

    • Removed exported function parseExcelDate() because there seems to be no need to have it exported.

  • If you were using schema parameter:

    • Removed schema parameter. Instead, use exported function parseData(data, schema) to map data to an array of objects.
      • Old: import readXlsxFile from "read-excel-file" and then const { rows, errors } = await readXlsxFile(..., { schema })
      • New: import { readSheet, parseData } from "read-excel-file/browser" and then const result = parseData(await readSheet(...), schema)
        • The result of the function is an array where each element represents a "data row" and has shape { object, errors }.
          • Depending on whether there were any errors when parsing a given "data row", either object or errors property will be undefined.
          • The errors don't have a row property anymore because it could be derived from "data row" number.
            • In version 9.x, the row property has been re-added, so consider migrating straight to 9.x.
          • In version 9.x, the returned result of parseData() has been changed back to { errors, objects }, so consider migrating straight to 9.x. In that case, if there're no errors, errors will be undefined; otherwise, errors will be a non-empty array and objects will be undefined.

... (truncated)

  • 4025e13 Edited some parseData() tests
  • 59d1ec6 9.0.0
  • 2cc9538 Refactored parseData() function
  • d661fc7 Renamed ReadFileResult to Sheet
  • 6fab8f0 Merge branch 'fix/expose-ReadFileResult' into 'master'
  • 3cf91c7 fix: expose ReadFileResult
  • See full diff in compare view

    • In a schema, a nested object could be declared as: { required: true/false, schema: { ... } }. This is still true but the required flag is now only allowed to be either undefined or false, so true value is not allowed. The reason is quite simple. If a nested object as a whole is marked as required: true, and then it happens to be empty, a "required" error should be returned for it. But that error would also have to include a column title, and a nested object simply can't be pinned down to a single column in a sheet because it is by definition spread over multiple columns. So instead of marking a nested object as a whole with required: true, mark the specific required properties of it.

8.0.0 / 11.03.2026

  • If you were using the default exported function:

    • Renamed the default exported function to a named exported function readSheet.
      • Old: import readExcelFile from "read-excel-file/browser"
      • New: import { readSheet } from "read-excel-file/browser"
      • And same for other exports like "read-excel-file/node", etc.
    • The default exported function now returns a different kind of result. Specifically, now it returns all available sheets — an array of objects: [{ sheet: "Sheet 1", data: [['a1','b1','c1'],['a2','b2','c2']] }, ...].
    • The default exported function used to return sheet names when passed getSheets: true parameter. Now, instead of that, the default exported function just returns all available sheets, from which one could get the sheet names.

  • If you were using readSheetNames() function:

    • Removed exported function readSheetNames(). Use the default exported function instead. The default exported function now returns all sheets.

  • If you were using parseExcelDate() function:

    • Removed exported function parseExcelDate() because there seems to be no need to have it exported.

  • If you were using schema parameter:

    • Removed schema parameter. Instead, use exported function parseData(data, schema) to map data to an array of objects.
      • Old: import readXlsxFile from "read-excel-file" and then const { rows, errors } = await readXlsxFile(..., { schema })
      • New: import { readSheet, parseData } from "read-excel-file/browser" and then const result = parseData(await readSheet(...), schema)
        • The result of the function is an array where each element represents a "data row" and has shape { object, errors }.
          • Depending on whether there were any errors when parsing a given "data row", either object or errors property will be undefined.
          • The errors don't have a row property anymore because it could be derived from "data row" number.
            • In version 9.x, the row property has been re-added, so consider migrating straight to 9.x.
          • In version 9.x, the returned result of parseData() has been changed back to { errors, objects }, so consider migrating straight to 9.x. In that case, if there're no errors, errors will be undefined; otherwise, errors will be a non-empty array and objects will be undefined.

... (truncated)

  • 4025e13 Edited some parseData() tests
  • 59d1ec6 9.0.0
  • 2cc9538 Refactored parseData() function
  • d661fc7 Renamed ReadFileResult to Sheet
  • 6fab8f0 Merge branch 'fix/expose-ReadFileResult' into 'master'
  • 3cf91c7 fix: expose ReadFileResult
  • See full diff in compare view

## Summary
6-line addition to `web/AGENTS.md` capturing three TypeScript
conventions that:
1. The now-deleted `.claude/skills/nextjs-react-typescript` skill
correctly identified
2. The codebase actually follows
3. Aren't lint-enforced (so worth writing down explicitly)

```markdown

Not lint-enforced, but consistently followed across `src/`:
- Functional components only (legacy class holdouts: `ErrorBoundary`, `chat/GenClawClient`)
- Prefer `interface` over `type` for object shapes; use `type` for unions / mapped types / transforms
- Avoid `enum` — use `as const` maps or string literal unions (codebase has zero enums)
```

— natural flow from React-specific to TypeScript-general to test
guidance.

mismatch. The `nextjs-react-typescript` skill was mostly generic Next.js
advice with a few wrong specifics (recommended `nuqs` which isn't in
deps; "minimize 'use client'" doesn't match the 76% client-component
reality on Cloudflare Workers + OpenNext). But three of its claims
**were** verified correct by grep against `src/` — those land here.

fix(ci): allow srp-claude-assistant in codex-action write-access gate (#1543)

## Summary
On Dependabot PRs that need a lockfile refresh,
`dependabot-lockfile-refresh.yml` (#1529) pushes the refreshed
`pnpm-lock.yaml` via APP_TOKEN. That fires a new
`pull_request[synchronize]` with `github.actor ==
'srp-claude-assistant[bot]'`, which:

- **Claude review** (via srp-actions reusable) handles fine —
`allowed_bots: \"srp-claude-assistant,github-actions\"` is already set,
so the reviewer runs and approves.
- **Codex review** dies in `openai/codex-action`'s built-in
`check-write-access` step: the App bot has no repo collaborator role
(\`permission level 'none'\`), and the action's default `allow-bots:
false` rejects it.

Reproduced on PR #1541.

## Fix
Set `allow-bot-users: srp-claude-assistant` on the codex-action input.
That's the action's intended escape hatch for trusted in-house bot
identities — same shape as the `allowed_bots` knob on the Claude side.

The original `actor != 'dependabot[bot]'` skip is preserved: it still
correctly skips the dependabot-triggered run, where GitHub's built-in
policy blocks secrets so codex would fail anyway.

Net effect: Dependabot PRs are codex-reviewed on the post-refresh sha
(the sha that actually merges), at parity with the Claude reviewer.

## Why two commits
First commit was an over-engineered predicate change. Force-push is
denied locally, so the second commit reverts that and applies the
minimal one-input fix instead. Squash-merge to keep the final history
clean.

## Test plan
- [ ] After merge, on PR #1541: comment \`@dependabot rebase\`. The new
lockfile-refresh push triggers codex-review, which now passes the
write-access gate.
- [ ] Confirm codex-review posts a real review comment (`## 🤖 Codex
Review`) on the post-refresh sha.

chore(agents): make AGENTS.md the file, CLAUDE.md the symlink at every path (#1531)

## Summary
Adopt the [agents.md](https://agents.md/) cross-tool convention with
**single source of truth** semantics: at every path that previously had
`CLAUDE.md`, the file is renamed to `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` becomes
a relative symlink pointing at its `AGENTS.md` sibling.

| Path | AGENTS.md | CLAUDE.md |
|---|---|---|
| `/` (root) | regular file (was CLAUDE.md content) | symlink →
`AGENTS.md` |
| `web/` | regular file | symlink → `AGENTS.md` |
| `services/claw-interface/` | regular file | symlink → `AGENTS.md` |
| `ios/ZooClaw/` | regular file | symlink → `AGENTS.md` |
| `web/src/app/landing/` | regular file | symlink → `AGENTS.md` |
| `web/src/app/[locale]/admin/` | regular file | symlink → `AGENTS.md` |

After merge, both `cat CLAUDE.md` and `cat AGENTS.md` return the same
content at every level — no duplicate maintenance, no drift.

## Why
- Other agent tools (Codex, Cursor, etc.) discover project guidance via
`AGENTS.md` per the agents.md convention; Claude Code reads `CLAUDE.md`.
The symlink lets one file serve both without duplication.
- Internal cross-references like "see `web/CLAUDE.md`" continue to
resolve, because `web/CLAUDE.md` is now a symlink to `web/AGENTS.md`
(same content). Heading text (`# CLAUDE.md - <title>`) and these
internal refs are kept verbatim — localizing them to AGENTS.md naming is
left for a follow-up if desired.

## Compatibility notes
- **CI workflows**
(`.github/workflows/{claude-arch-review,claude-develop,codex-review}.yaml`)
reference `CLAUDE.md` only inside prompt text instructing Claude/Codex
to read it. File reads follow symlinks transparently — no workflow
changes needed.
- **Symlinks in git** are stored as mode `120000` blobs (target string
as content). They work natively on macOS/Linux and on the project's
Linux dev container / Ubuntu CI. Windows is not in the dev surface
(`.devcontainer/` and `.github/` have no Windows references). If a
future Windows contributor joins, they may need `git config
core.symlinks true` + dev-mode/admin.
- **Closes** the gap left by #1530 (closed) — this is the clean
replacement, without the Dify-specific skill packs.

## History on this branch
Branch has two commits because the first one (an initial divergent
`AGENTS.md`) was kept rather than force-pushed. The cumulative diff is
the symlink-migration shape in the table above.

## Test plan
- [ ] CI green (no `web/**` or `services/**` source changed; lint/test
paths read `*.md` only via Claude/Codex prompts)
- [ ] After clone + checkout: `cat CLAUDE.md` and `cat AGENTS.md` show
identical content at root and at each sub-path
- [ ] `git ls-files -s CLAUDE.md` shows mode `120000` (symlink) on each
`CLAUDE.md`

message:
chore(skills): remove three Dify-vendored Claude skills that don't fit this repo (#1542)

## Summary
Three of the four committed skills under `.claude/skills/` are vendored
straight from Dify's frontend skill pack and reference things that don't
exist in this repo. Removing them so they stop misleading Claude Code
agents in this project.

| Skill | Why it doesn't fit |
|---|---|
| `frontend-testing` | Hard-codes Dify's project name,
`web/docs/test.md`, `pnpm analyze-component` script,
`web/__mocks__/provider-context.ts` — none exist here. Any agent
following it errors immediately. |
| `frontend-code-review` | Assumes React Flow hooks (Dify's visual
workflow builder); this repo doesn't use React Flow. |
| `frontend-design` | Targets "Codex" by name (not Claude); prescribes
`NEVER use Inter / Roboto / Arial` — directly conflicts with Tailwind's
default sans stack and would fight the existing design system. |

This is the `.claude/` counterpart of the same cleanup that was already
done for the `.agents/` tree (PR #1530, closed when we realized the same
skills shouldn't exist there either; superseded by the symlink-based
AGENTS.md migration in #1531).

## Not in this PR
The fourth skill, **`nextjs-react-typescript`**, also has stack-fit
issues:
- Recommends Shadcn UI / Radix UI which aren't in this stack (CLAUDE.md
lists Tailwind only)
- Pushes "minimize 'use client', favor RSC" patterns that need separate
evaluation against this repo's Cloudflare Workers deploy target (Workers
≠ Vercel for RSC support)

Left in place pending separate review — flagged here for visibility.

## Why PR-size bypass
Pure deletion of 16 vendored files; no review value in line-by-line.
Pushed with `SKIP_PR_SIZE_CHECK=1` (same approach used for #1530's
predecessor and the #1531 force-push).

## Test plan
- [ ] CI green
- [ ] After merge: `Skill` tool no longer surfaces frontend-testing /
frontend-code-review / frontend-design from `.claude/skills/`
- [ ] Followup decision on `nextjs-react-typescript` (separate PR if
removed)

ci(dependabot): ignore eslint-plugin-react-hooks majors (#1540)

## Summary

Adds `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` to dependabot's
`version-update:semver-major` ignore list — same coordinated-migration
pattern as existing `next` / `eslint` / `eslint-config-next` / `jsdom` /
`stripe` / `typescript` entries.

PR #1533 (now closed) surfaced a 5.2.0 → 7.1.1 cross-major bump that:
- Removes `flat/recommended` and `recommended-latest-legacy` configs
(would break our flat config without rework)
- Turns React Compiler rules on by default (introduces a wave of new
lint errors against existing code)

## Why a separate PR

This was originally bundled into #1535 but landed after that PR was
merged.

## Tracking the actual upgrade

Issue #1539 holds the upgrade plan — when that work lands, the
lift-checklist there includes removing this ignore.

message:
docs(spec): Python dependency locking design (post cachetools 7.1.0 incident) (#1538)

## Summary
Spec PR — **doc only, no code, no execution**. Per the project
convention in CLAUDE.md ("complex features/refactors: write a design
spec in `docs/superpowers/specs/`") and the user's "Plan doc first"
preference, this PR delivers the design and stops. Execution PRs follow
once the approach is approved.

## What this spec covers
- **Trigger**: 2026-05-02 timeline showing how `cachetools` v7.1.0
(released 2026-05-01 with new typeshed-based stubs) broke
`claw-interface-quality / lint-and-typecheck` across every PR touching
`services/` within ~24h.
- **Current state**: maps the **five dependency entry points**
(requirements.txt, pyproject.toml dynamic deps, the placeholder uv.lock,
`.devcontainer/postCreateCommand.sh`, and the
`SerendipityOneInc/srp-actions` reusable workflow at
`python-code-quality-v3.yml@main`).
- **Approaches surveyed**:
- **A**: uv-native `uv.lock` (PEP 621, `[project] dependencies`
migration)
- **B**: `uv pip compile` style `requirements.lock` (pip-tools-like,
recommended)
- **C**: pin-only without lockfile (rejected — doesn't address
transitive drift)
- **Recommendation**: **Approach B first** (smallest blast radius, keeps
current dependabot pip ecosystem; lockfile first lands in devcontainer
to validate, then CI), **A as future option**.
- **Rollout**: 6-PR plan, scoped per the project's "PR per step"
pattern.
- **Open questions**: lockfile naming (`requirements.lock` vs `uv.lock`
vs `requirements.txt.lock`), dependabot recompile mechanism (CI
strict-check vs auto-recompile bot), srp-actions release strategy,
dev-deps lock necessity.

## Why a spec instead of just opening the lockfile PR
1. CI install path lives in **`SerendipityOneInc/srp-actions`** — not
single-repo
2. `pyproject.toml` uses `dynamic = ["dependencies"]` → real `uv lock`
requires migration
3. dependabot ecosystem coordination needs a written agreement
4. Five entry points means the wrong sequencing creates breakage
5. Direct `uv lock` on current `pyproject.toml` already failed locally
(setuptools build backend error) — proving the work isn't trivial

## Related
- #1536 — short-term `cast()` workaround for the cachetools 7.1.0
type-stub incident (the immediate trigger)
- #1531 / #1518 — both blocked by the same incident at the time of
writing

## Test plan
- [x] Spec doc renders cleanly on GitHub
- [ ] Maintainer review: agree on Approach B (or redirect to A / C)
- [ ] Resolve naming + dependabot questions in review comments before
opening execution PRs

pr_body:
## Summary
Spec PR — **doc only, no code, no execution**. Per the project convention in CLAUDE.md ("complex features/refactors: write a design spec in `docs/superpowers/specs/`") and the user's "Plan doc first" preference, this PR delivers the design and stops. Execution PRs follow once the approach is approved.

## What this spec covers
- **Trigger**: 2026-05-02 timeline showing how `cachetools` v7.1.0 (released 2026-05-01 with new typeshed-based stubs) broke `claw-interface-quality / lint-and-typecheck` across every PR touching `services/` within ~24h.
- **Current state**: maps the **five dependency entry points** (requirements.txt, pyproject.toml dynamic deps, the placeholder uv.lock, `.devcontainer/postCreateCommand.sh`, and the `SerendipityOneInc/srp-actions` reusable workflow at `python-code-quality-v3.yml@main`).
- **Approaches surveyed**:
  - **A**: uv-native `uv.lock` (PEP 621, `[project] dependencies` migration)
  - **B**: `uv pip compile` style `requirements.lock` (pip-tools-like, recommended)
  - **C**: pin-only without lockfile (rejected — doesn't address transitive drift)
- **Recommendation**: **Approach B first** (smallest blast radius, keeps current dependabot pip ecosystem; lockfile first lands in devcontainer to validate, then CI), **A as future option**.
- **Rollout**: 6-PR plan, scoped per the project's "PR per step" pattern.
- **Open questions**: lockfile naming (`requirements.lock` vs `uv.lock` vs `requirements.txt.lock`), dependabot recompile mechanism (CI strict-check vs auto-recompile bot), srp-actions release strategy, dev-deps lock necessity.

## Why a spec instead of just opening the lockfile PR
1. CI install path lives in **`SerendipityOneInc/srp-actions`** — not single-repo
2. `pyproject.toml` uses `dynamic = ["dependencies"]` → real `uv lock` requires migration
3. dependabot ecosystem coordination needs a written agreement
4. Five entry points means the wrong sequencing creates breakage
5. Direct `uv lock` on current `pyproject.toml` already failed locally (setuptools build backend error) — proving the work isn't trivial

## Related
- #1536 — short-term `cast()` workaround for the cachetools 7.1.0 type-stub incident (the immediate trigger)
- #1531 / #1518 — both blocked by the same incident at the time of writing

## Test plan
- [x] Spec doc renders cleanly on GitHub
- [ ] Maintainer review: agree on Approach B (or redirect to A / C)
- [ ] Resolve naming + dependabot questions in review comments before opening execution PRs

message:
chore(deps): update cryptography requirement from >=46.0.7 to >=47.0.0 in /services/claw-interface (#1518) Updates the requirements on [cryptography](https://github.com/pyca/cryptography) to permit the latest version.
Changelog

Sourced from cryptography's changelog.

47.0.0 - 2026-04-24


* Support for Python 3.8 is deprecated and will be removed in the next
  ``cryptography`` release.
* **BACKWARDS INCOMPATIBLE:** Support for binary elliptic curves
  (``SECT*`` classes) has been removed. These curves are rarely used and
  have additional security considerations that make them undesirable.
* **BACKWARDS INCOMPATIBLE:** Support for OpenSSL 1.1.x has been
removed.
OpenSSL 3.0.0 or later is now required. LibreSSL, BoringSSL, and AWS-LC
  continue to be supported.
* **BACKWARDS INCOMPATIBLE:** Dropped support for LibreSSL < 4.1.
* **BACKWARDS INCOMPATIBLE:** Loading keys with unsupported algorithms
or
  keys with unsupported explicit curve encodings now raises
  :class:`~cryptography.exceptions.UnsupportedAlgorithm` instead of
  ``ValueError``. This change affects

:func:`~cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization.load_pem_private_key`,

:func:`~cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization.load_der_private_key`,

:func:`~cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization.load_pem_public_key`,

:func:`~cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization.load_der_public_key`,
  and :meth:`~cryptography.x509.Certificate.public_key` when called on
  certificates with unsupported public key algorithms.
* **BACKWARDS INCOMPATIBLE:** When parsing elliptic curve private keys,
we now
reject keys that incorrectly encode a private key of the wrong length
because
such keys are impossible to process in a constant-time manner. We do not
believe keys with this problem are in wide use, however we may revert
this
  change based on the feedback we receive.
* Deprecated passing 64-bit (8-byte) and 128-bit (16-byte) keys to
:class:`~cryptography.hazmat.decrepit.ciphers.algorithms.TripleDES`. In
a
future release, only 192-bit (24-byte) keys will be accepted. Users
should
expand shorter keys themselves (e.g., for single DES: ``key + key +
key``,
  for two-key: ``key + key[:8]``).
* Updated the minimum supported Rust version (MSRV) to 1.83.0, from
1.74.0.
* Support for ``x86_64`` macOS (including publishing wheels) is
deprecated
and will be removed in the next release. We will switch to publishing an
  ``arm64`` only wheel for macOS.
* Support for 32-bit Windows (including publishing wheels) is deprecated
  and will be removed in the next release. Users should move to a 64-bit
  Python installation.
* ``public_bytes`` and ``private_bytes`` methods on keys now raise
``TypeError`` (instead of ``ValueError``) if an invalid encoding is
provided
  for the given ``format``.
* Moved :class:`~cryptography.hazmat.decrepit.ciphers.modes.CFB`,
  :class:`~cryptography.hazmat.decrepit.ciphers.modes.OFB`, and
  :class:`~cryptography.hazmat.decrepit.ciphers.modes.CFB8` into
:doc:`/hazmat/decrepit/index` and deprecated them in the ``modes``
module.
  They will be removed from the ``modes`` module in 49.0.0.
* Moved
:class:`~cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.algorithms.Camellia`
into :doc:`/hazmat/decrepit/index` and deprecated it in the ``cipher``
module.
  It will be removed from the ``cipher`` module in 49.0.0.
</tr></table>

... (truncated)

author: Chris@ZooClaw
message:
fix(claw-interface): bind TTLCache K/V types via cast for cachetools 7.x (#1536)

## Summary
**Unblocks `claw-interface-quality / lint-and-typecheck` for every PR
touching `services/`** — currently failing on at least #1518
(cryptography bump in merge queue) and #1531 (AGENTS.md migration).

## Problem
cachetools 7.x typed `TTLCache` as `Generic[_KT, _VT, _T]` with `_T`
defaulting to `float` (the timer return type). The existing annotation
at `app/connectors/google.py:17`:

```python
_GOOGLE_JWKS_CACHE: cachetools.TTLCache[str, Any] = cachetools.TTLCache(maxsize=1, ttl=3600)
```

resolves the declared type to `TTLCache[str, Any, float]` (default
applied), but the constructor returns `TTLCache[_KT2@__init__,
_VT2@__init__, float]` (K/V unbound, since they can't be inferred from
`maxsize=1, ttl=3600` kwargs). Pyright reports:

```
app/connectors/google.py:17:53 - error: Type "TTLCache[_KT2@__init__, _VT2@__init__, float]" is not assignable to declared type "TTLCache[str, Any, float]"
1 error, 0 warnings, 0 informations
```

## Fix
Wrap the constructor in `typing.cast` to bind K=str, V=Any explicitly.
No runtime behavior change.

```python
_GOOGLE_JWKS_CACHE: cachetools.TTLCache[str, Any] = cast(
    "cachetools.TTLCache[str, Any]",
    cachetools.TTLCache(maxsize=1, ttl=3600),
)
```

## Why this surfaced now
`cachetools` was bumped from `>=5.0.0` to `>=7.0.6` in #1207. Earlier PR
runs apparently picked up an older lockfile resolution; recent PRs are
now resolving the 7.x stubs that ship the third type parameter.

## Test plan
- [x] Local pyright pre-commit hook: `Pyright Type Check ...... Passed`
- [ ] CI `claw-interface-quality / lint-and-typecheck` green on this PR
- [ ] After merge: rebase #1518 / #1531 onto main and verify their CI
clears

author: Chris@ZooClaw
message:
chore(husky): fall back to $PATH for pre-commit when venv path absent (#1534)

## Summary
The husky `pre-commit` hook hard-codes
`/home/node/.venvs/claw-interface/bin/pre-commit` (devcontainer venv)
and only honors `PRE_COMMIT_BIN` as an override. Running `git commit` on
the **host** (outside the devcontainer — e.g. macOS via brew/conda)
errors out:

```
Error: pre-commit not found at /home/node/.venvs/claw-interface/bin/pre-commit — backend pre-commit checks cannot run
  Set PRE_COMMIT_BIN to override the default path
```

Add a `command -v pre-commit` fallback so any host-installed
`pre-commit` (brew, conda, pipx, pip --user) is auto-discovered when the
configured path isn't executable.

## Resolution order (after this PR)
1. `$PRE_COMMIT_BIN` env (explicit override) — unchanged
2. `/home/node/.venvs/claw-interface/bin/pre-commit` (devcontainer venv)
— unchanged primary path
3. `command -v pre-commit` (`$PATH` lookup) — **new fallback**

Devcontainer behavior is identical (step 2 still wins). Host users no
longer need to remember `PRE_COMMIT_BIN=...` before every commit.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash -n .husky/pre-commit` — syntax OK
- [ ] Inside devcontainer: stage a `services/claw-interface/` change,
commit, confirm hook still uses the venv path (printout: `Running Python
pre-commit hooks...`)
- [ ] On macOS host with `pre-commit` installed (e.g. via brew/conda):
stage a `services/claw-interface/` change, commit, confirm hook
auto-resolves and runs successfully
- [ ] On a host with no `pre-commit` at all: confirm error message now
suggests installing pre-commit (not just the env-var workaround)

author: Chris@ZooClaw
message:
fix(ci): dependabot lockfile refresh needs --no-frozen-lockfile (#1535)

## Summary

Bug from #1529 caught live on the first real dependabot PR (#1532): pnpm
10 auto-enables `frozen-lockfile=true` whenever `CI=true` is set (every
GitHub Actions runner). So even the refresh step (`pnpm install --filter
./web --config.minimumReleaseAge=0`) was running as a frozen install,
hitting the same `ERR_PNPM_OUTDATED_LOCKFILE` the probe step had already
failed on, and never writing a new lockfile.

Pass `--no-frozen-lockfile` explicitly on the refresh step.

The probe step keeps `--frozen-lockfile` — that's the contract we want
there (we WANT it to fail when the lockfile is stale, that's the signal
that triggers the refresh path).

## How this slipped through #1529

I tested the refresh step locally where `CI` isn't set, so
`frozen-lockfile` defaulted to false and the install actually
re-resolved. The CI default flip is one of those env-injected behaviors
that only surfaces on a real runner.

## Test plan

- [x] #1532 lockfile manually refreshed and pushed (CI green)
- [ ] Merge → next dependabot PR (or a re-trigger of #1532 by reopening)
exercises the workflow end-to-end with the fix

author: Chris@ZooClaw
message:
refactor(web): RQ v2 PR-b2 — useClawSettings main read switches to useQuery (#1523)

## Summary

- Migrate `useClawSettings` settings + locale fetch from `useEffect +
useState` to `useQuery`; mutations write through `setQueryData` for
optimistic UX. Hook facade unchanged (21 fields), so consumers
(`ClawSettingsClient`, `AgentSettingsPopover`) untouched.
- New `openclawKeys.clawSettings(uid)` entry — uid-keyed for
cross-account isolation (`null` only on logged-out renders where the
query is disabled).
- Side fetches (`fetchLogs` / `fetchResources` / `fetchUsage`), polling,
and redeploy stay imperative — they're not in spec's anti-pattern
baseline and migrating them would inflate this PR. Deferred to a
follow-up.

## Why no `invalidateQueries` after mutations

The API wrapper layer (`lib/api/openclaw-settings.ts`) maintains its own
30s throttle cache (`_settingsCache`) and clears it only **after** the
PUT response. Letting RQ refetch immediately after the optimistic write
could briefly flash the pre-mutation value before resettling on the new
state. The optimistic `setQueryData` is the source of truth for the same
render; the next user-triggered `loadSettings()` or focus refetch
reflects the server state.

A future PR can collapse the wrapper's `_settingsCache` into RQ — they
currently coexist as two layers, both invalidated on mutation.

## What is *not* changed

- Two raw `getClawSettings` callers (`SideNav.tsx`, `GenClawClient.tsx`)
still bypass RQ — tracked under spec PR-c (`claw-settings/` sweep).
- `_settingsCache` / `_settingsInflight` 30s throttle in API wrapper.
- `pollUntilReady` lifted to module scope (no closure deps) to keep the
hook under the 300-line function-length lint cap.

## Test plan

- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` clean
- [x] `pnpm lint` clean
- [x] `npx vitest run tests/unit/hooks/useClawSettings.unit.spec.ts` —
26/26 pass
- [x] `npx vitest run
tests/unit/app/claw-settings/ClawSettingsClient.unit.spec.tsx
tests/unit/components/AgentSettingsPopover.unit.spec.tsx` — 33/33 pass
(consumers stay green via unchanged facade)
- [ ] Smoke: `/claw-settings` page loads, save model / timezone /
identity each show optimistic update
- [ ] Smoke: cross-account switch — settings cache miss on uid change

Refs: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-react-query-migration-v2.md`
PR series: #1455 (PR-a / b1 merged) → this (PR-b2)

author: Chris@ZooClaw
message:
ci(dependabot): auto-refresh pnpm-lock.yaml + ignore typescript major (#1529)

## Summary

- New workflow `.github/workflows/dependabot-lockfile-refresh.yml`
detects dependabot PRs where `web/package.json` was bumped but
`pnpm-lock.yaml` wasn't refreshed (recurring symptom of
pnpm-workspace.yaml's `minimumReleaseAge: 10080` guard silently dropping
the lockfile write in dependabot's runner). It probes with
`--frozen-lockfile`, falls back to `--config.minimumReleaseAge=0`
install, verifies only the lockfile changed, and pushes back via GitHub
App token so CI re-triggers and the dependabot PR goes green on first
attempt.
- Add `typescript` to dependabot.yml's `/web` ignore list for
`version-update:semver-major` — same pattern as existing `next` /
`eslint` / `jsdom` / `stripe` ignores; TS major needs a coordinated
migration window.

## Recent PRs that motivated this

- #1519 (group bump, manually fixed by pushing refreshed lockfile)
- #1520 (wrangler, same)
- #1521 (typescript 6 major — closed under the new ignore)

## Security notes (workflow)

- Uses `pull_request_target` to access secrets + write — necessary
because dependabot's `pull_request` runs are read-only and secrets-less.
Compensated by:
- Three-layer `if:` gate (`actor == dependabot[bot]` + same-repo +
`head.ref` starts with `dependabot/`)
- Step-level `case "$BRANCH" in dependabot/*) ;; *) exit 1 ;;` redundant
check before the push
- Only ever runs `pnpm install` (no arbitrary repo scripts via
`--ignore-scripts`)
- `git diff --name-only` whitelist before commit (rejects unexpected
file changes from a hostile transitive postinstall)
- All `\${{ github.event.* }}` consumed via `env:` indirection, never
inlined into shell commands
- App-token push is the mechanism that retriggers CI (default
`GITHUB_TOKEN` push deliberately doesn't, by GitHub's loop-prevention
design)

pr_body:
## Summary

- New workflow `.github/workflows/dependabot-lockfile-refresh.yml` detects dependabot PRs where `web/package.json` was bumped but `pnpm-lock.yaml` wasn't refreshed (recurring symptom of pnpm-workspace.yaml's `minimumReleaseAge: 10080` guard silently dropping the lockfile write in dependabot's runner). It probes with `--frozen-lockfile`, falls back to `--config.minimumReleaseAge=0` install, verifies only the lockfile changed, and pushes back via GitHub App token so CI re-triggers and the dependabot PR goes green on first attempt.
- Add `typescript` to dependabot.yml's `/web` ignore list for `version-update:semver-major` — same pattern as existing `next` / `eslint` / `jsdom` / `stripe` ignores; TS major needs a coordinated migration window.

## Recent PRs that motivated this

- #1519 (group bump, manually fixed by pushing refreshed lockfile)
- #1520 (wrangler, same)
- #1521 (typescript 6 major — closed under the new ignore)

## Security notes (workflow)

- Uses `pull_request_target` to access secrets + write — necessary because dependabot's `pull_request` runs are read-only and secrets-less. Compensated by:
  - Three-layer `if:` gate (`actor == dependabot[bot]` + same-repo + `head.ref` starts with `dependabot/`)
  - Step-level `case "$BRANCH" in dependabot/*) ;; *) exit 1 ;;` redundant check before the push
  - Only ever runs `pnpm install` (no arbitrary repo scripts via `--ignore-scripts`)
  - `git diff --name-only` whitelist before commit (rejects unexpected file changes from a hostile transitive postinstall)
  - All `\${{ github.event.* }}` consumed via `env:` indirection, never inlined into shell commands
- App-token push is the mechanism that retriggers CI (default `GITHUB_TOKEN` push deliberately doesn't, by GitHub's loop-prevention design)
- `concurrency: cancel-in-progress: true` cuts the dependabot-rebase race window; head-SHA recheck before push is defense in depth
- Failure path posts a PR comment so a human is paged instead of silently red CI

## Test plan

- [ ] Merge → next dependabot run (weekly) opens a PR → workflow runs → CI passes on first try
- [ ] Or manually reopen a closed dependabot PR to trigger \`reopened\` event before next weekly cycle
- [ ] Verify success path (lockfile already valid) is fast (~30s, exits at \`frozen\` probe)
- [ ] Verify failure-mode comment fires by simulating with a bad \`pnpm-lock.yaml\`

Updates `@opennextjs/cloudflare` from 1.19.3 to 1.19.4
Release notes

Sourced from @​opennextjs/cloudflare's releases.

@​opennextjs/cloudflare@​1.19.4

Patch Changes

  • #1221 a2679bf Thanks @​mushan0x0! - Stop bundling @vercel/og (and its ~1.4 MiB resvg.wasm) when the app does not use it.

    Next.js's externalImport helper keeps a dynamic import("next/dist/compiled/@vercel/og/index.edge.js") in the emitted handler even for apps that never use ImageResponse / opengraph-image. Previously this module was marked as external when useOg was false, which left Wrangler to resolve and bundle it — pulling in ~800 KiB of JS plus resvg.wasm and pushing many Workers over the Cloudflare free-tier 3 MiB gzip limit.

    When useOg is false, the edge entry is now aliased to the existing throw.js shim, so the unreachable dynamic import resolves to a tiny module and the real @vercel/og library is no longer pulled into the Worker bundle.

  • #1208 2c5b472 Thanks @​edmundhung! - Use OPEN_NEXT_BUILD_ID instead of NEXT_BUILD_ID in the cache keys.

    As of Next 16.2 NEXT_BUILD_ID is a fixed value when deploymentId is set explicitly.

    See opennextjs/opennextjs-aws#1144

  • #1193 1e8d232 Thanks @​conico974! - Fix tag cache stale logic

Updates `@opennextjs/cloudflare` from 1.19.3 to 1.19.4
Release notes

Sourced from @​opennextjs/cloudflare's releases.

@​opennextjs/cloudflare@​1.19.4

Patch Changes

  • #1221 a2679bf Thanks @​mushan0x0! - Stop bundling @vercel/og (and its ~1.4 MiB resvg.wasm) when the app does not use it.

    Next.js's externalImport helper keeps a dynamic import("next/dist/compiled/@vercel/og/index.edge.js") in the emitted handler even for apps that never use ImageResponse / opengraph-image. Previously this module was marked as external when useOg was false, which left Wrangler to resolve and bundle it — pulling in ~800 KiB of JS plus resvg.wasm and pushing many Workers over the Cloudflare free-tier 3 MiB gzip limit.

    When useOg is false, the edge entry is now aliased to the existing throw.js shim, so the unreachable dynamic import resolves to a tiny module and the real @vercel/og library is no longer pulled into the Worker bundle.

  • #1208 2c5b472 Thanks @​edmundhung! - Use OPEN_NEXT_BUILD_ID instead of NEXT_BUILD_ID in the cache keys.

    As of Next 16.2 NEXT_BUILD_ID is a fixed value when deploymentId is set explicitly.

    See opennextjs/opennextjs-aws#1144

  • #1193 1e8d232 Thanks @​conico974! - Fix tag cache stale logic

Release notes

Sourced from @​sentry/cloudflare's releases.

10.50.0

Important Changes

  • feat(effect): Support v4 beta (#20394)

    The @sentry/effect integration now supports Effect v4 beta, enabling Sentry instrumentation for the latest Effect framework version. Read more in the Effect SDK readme.

  • feat(hono): Add @sentry/hono/bun for Bun runtime (#20355)

    A new @sentry/hono/bun entry point adds first-class support for running Hono applications instrumented with Sentry on the Bun runtime. Read more in the Hono SDK readme.

  • feat(replay): Add replayStart/replayEnd client lifecycle hooks (#20369)

    New replayStart and replayEnd client lifecycle hooks let you react to replay session start and end events in your application.

chore(deps-dev): bump wrangler from 3.114.17 to 4.83.0 in /web (#1520) Bumps [wrangler](https://github.com/cloudflare/workers-sdk/tree/HEAD/packages/wrangler) from 3.114.17 to 4.83.0.
Release notes

Sourced from wrangler's releases.

wrangler@4.83.0

Minor Changes

  • #13391 60565dd Thanks @​mikenomitch! - Mark wrangler containers commands as stable

    This changes the status of the Containers CLI from open beta to stable. Wrangler no longer shows [open beta] labels or beta warning text for wrangler containers commands, so the help output matches the feature's current availability.

  • #13311 6cbcdeb Thanks @​ryanking13! - JS files imported by the Python Workers runtime SDK are now handled as ESM modules.

    This is not a user-facing change, but Python Workers users should update their wrangler version to make sure to get Python workers SDK working properly.

Patch Changes

  • #13450 6f63eaa Thanks @​petebacondarwin! - Fix POST/PUT requests with non-2xx responses throwing "fetch failed"

    Previously, sending a POST or PUT request that received a non-2xx response (e.g. 401, 400, 403) would throw a TypeError: fetch failed error. This was caused by an undici bug where isTraversableNavigable() incorrectly returned true, causing the 401 credential-retry block to execute in Node.js and fail on stream-backed request bodies. This has been fixed upstream in undici v7.24.8, so we've bumped our dependency and removed the previous pnpm patch workaround.

  • #13447 aef9825 Thanks @​dependabot! - Update dependencies of "miniflare", "wrangler"

    The following dependency versions have been updated:

    Dependency From To
    workerd 1.20260410.1 1.20260413.1

  • #13475 eaaa728 Thanks @​dependabot! - Update dependencies of "miniflare", "wrangler"

    The following dependency versions have been updated:

    Dependency From To
    workerd 1.20260413.1 1.20260415.1

  • #13386 5e5bbc1 Thanks @​mksglu! - Make startup network requests non-blocking on slow connections

    Wrangler makes network requests during startup (npm update check, request.cf data fetch) that previously blocked the CLI indefinitely on slow or degraded connections (airplane wifi, trains), causing 10+ second delays.

    • Update check: The banner now races the update check against a 100ms grace period. On a cache hit (most runs) the result resolves in <1ms via the I/O poll phase; on a cache miss the banner prints immediately without blocking. A 3s safety-net timeout caps the update-check library's auth-retry path.
    • request.cf fetch: The fetch to workers.cloudflare.com/cf.json now uses AbortSignal.timeout(3000), falling back to cached/default data on timeout.

  • #13469 07a918c Thanks @​1000hz! - wrangler preview no longer warns on inheritable binding types being missing from previews config.

  • #13463 90aee27 Thanks @​roerohan! - Remove unnecessary flagship:read OAuth scope

    The flagship:read scope is not needed since flagship:write already implies read access. This reduces the OAuth permissions requested during login to only what is required.

  • Updated dependencies [854d66c, 6f63eaa, aef9825, eaaa728, 58292f6, 5e5bbc1, d5ff5a4, 89c7829]:

message:
refactor(ci): arch-review 改用 issue body tracker 替代 comment 追加 (#1522)

## Summary

- **Issue body = single source of truth**: workflow 改写 issue body 而非追加
comment。`<!-- ARCH_REVIEW:* -->` markers 切分 INTRO / SUMMARY / FINDINGS /
RESOLVED 四段；marker 之外的 free text 一字不动（保留手工补充的 follow-up issues 等）。
- **Finding 状态分类**: still-present 保留 / resolved 划掉移到 Recently resolved
(8 周 trim) / 新发现标 🆕 NEW；F<N> ID + First seen 跨周持久化。
- **NEXT_ID 单调计数器**: `<!-- ARCH_REVIEW:NEXT_ID:N -->` marker 跨 run
单调递增，resolved bullet 8 周后被 trim 也不会让旧 ID 被复用。
- **Stale review comment minimize**: 每次跑结束 GraphQL
`minimizeComment(classifier:OUTDATED)` 把 fingerprint 匹配的旧 bot 输出折叠。
- **Body 拼装 inline Python**: merge + 8-周 trim + footer 唯一短语锚定去重 + 60k 字符
hard ceiling。

## Pre-merge cleanup (已完成 2026-05-02)

为避免 workflow 首次跑时把 legacy issue body + 全量历史评论拼成 prior_body.md（#365/#366
各 ~52KB，会触发 60k hard fail），先手工把 3 个 issue 迁移到结构化格式，让 workflow
仅需要"steady-state structured-only"代码路径：

| issue | active findings | NEXT_ID | minimized comments |
|---|---|---|---|
| #365 (iOS) | F1-F4 | F5 | 6 |
| #366 (claw-interface) | F1-F4 | F5 | 5 |
| #368 (web) | F1-F7 | F8 | 3 |

#368 的手工 progress table / Derived follow-up issues / Top-5 priorities /
Resolved-since-original 4 个 section 已迁到 RESOLVED_END 之后（manual_suffix
协议位置）。所有 14 条历史 bot 评论 minimize 为 OUTDATED。

由此 workflow 中可以删去整段 legacy 兼容代码（最后一个 commit），净减 -97 行。

## Test plan

- [ ] Dispatch 单 module（`modules = arch-review:web`），检查 #368 body 仍含
markers / title 刷新到当日 / 还在的 finding 标记保持 F-ID 不变 + First seen 不变 /
任何新发现从 F8+ 起编号 / minimize 是 noop（visible bot comments 已经 0 条）
- [ ] 间隔几分钟同 module 再 dispatch 一次，验证 idempotent
- [ ] 三 module 全跑，#365 / #366 同样验证
- [ ] 长度兜底：log 输出的 "Composed body: <N> bytes" <50000

author: Chris@ZooClaw
message:
chore(lint): pin eslint-plugin-react-hooks 5.2.0 as direct dev dep (PR 3 of #1524) (#1528)

## Summary
- Add `"eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "5.2.0"` to `web/package.json`
devDependencies (exact pin, no caret) so the plugin version is owned by
us, not implicitly controlled by `eslint-config-next` transitively.
- The exact-pin choice (vs `^5.2.0`) is intentional: PR 1/2/3 of #1524
are about converting implicit contracts into explicit ones, so the
strongest signal — every bump goes through a reviewed PR (Dependabot or
human) — fits the same spirit. This also lets us deliberately decide
when to opt into the React Compiler-aware `react-hooks/react-compiler`
rule (5.x feature) rather than inherit it on a Next bump.
- **Follow-up commit**: knip's dep-health hard gate caught the pin (no
source file imports the plugin since it's still loaded via
`next/core-web-vitals` transitive resolution). Added a parallel
`ignoreDependencies` entry alongside the existing `eslint-config-next`
exemption — same shape, same rationale.

## Behavior change
**None.** Verified by:
- `pnpm-lock.yaml` web importers block has the new direct dep at
`5.2.0`; **a single physical instance** of
`eslint-plugin-react-hooks@5.2.0` exists (pnpm content-addressable store
deduplicates same-version direct + transitive refs).
- `pnpm lint` and `pnpm lint:ci` (full orchestrator including knip) pass
locally with exit 0, no new rule violations.
- `npx eslint --print-config web/src/app/layout.tsx | jq '.rules'`
confirms `react-hooks/rules-of-hooks` and `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps`
both still resolve to severity `[2]` (error).

## Lockfile diff anatomy
The `pnpm-lock.yaml` diff is `+11 / -8`. Two distinct kinds of change:
1. **Intentional**: 3 new lines under
`importers.web.devDependencies.eslint-plugin-react-hooks` recording the
new direct-dep edge.
2. **Incidental noise** (4 hunks): pnpm 10.x peer-id cleanup.
Identifiers like
`eslint-plugin-import@2.32.0(@typescript-eslint/parser@8.57.1(...))(eslint@9.39.4(...))`
collapse to `eslint-plugin-import@2.32.0` once pnpm proves the peer
chain is unambiguous across all reference points. **No package version
changes** — verified `next@15.5.15` and `eslint-config-next@15.5.15` are
unchanged from `origin/main`.

If reviewers find the second category distracting we can split into a
"lockfile-cleanup-only" prep PR, but it is fully equivalent semantics so
consolidating is preferable.

## Out of scope
- `react-hooks/react-compiler` rule activation — needs a separate
decision tied to React 19 / Next 15 compiler rollout strategy.
- Switching `eslint.config.mjs` from
`FlatCompat.extends('next/core-web-vitals')` to direct `import
reactHooks from 'eslint-plugin-react-hooks'` + explicit `plugins: {
'react-hooks': reactHooks }` registration. Would risk
duplicate-plugin-name errors against `next/core-web-vitals` and is
unnecessary for the pinning goal. (Doing it would also remove the need
for the new knip exemption — that's a meaningful trade-off worth
deferring rather than rushing.)

## Test plan
- [ ] `pnpm --filter web lint` passes locally and in CI (`code-quality /
lint-and-test`).
- [ ] `pnpm --filter web lint:ci` passes (knip dep-health hard gate).
- [ ] `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` (CI default) succeeds with no
integrity drift.
- [ ] `pnpm ls eslint-plugin-react-hooks --depth Infinity` shows a

## Summary
- Add `"eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "5.2.0"` to `web/package.json` devDependencies (exact pin, no caret) so the plugin version is owned by us, not implicitly controlled by `eslint-config-next` transitively.
- The exact-pin choice (vs `^5.2.0`) is intentional: PR 1/2/3 of #1524 are about converting implicit contracts into explicit ones, so the strongest signal — every bump goes through a reviewed PR (Dependabot or human) — fits the same spirit. This also lets us deliberately decide when to opt into the React Compiler-aware `react-hooks/react-compiler` rule (5.x feature) rather than inherit it on a Next bump.
- **Follow-up commit**: knip's dep-health hard gate caught the pin (no source file imports the plugin since it's still loaded via `next/core-web-vitals` transitive resolution). Added a parallel `ignoreDependencies` entry alongside the existing `eslint-config-next` exemption — same shape, same rationale.

## Behavior change
**None.** Verified by:
- `pnpm-lock.yaml` web importers block has the new direct dep at `5.2.0`; **a single physical instance** of `eslint-plugin-react-hooks@5.2.0` exists (pnpm content-addressable store deduplicates same-version direct + transitive refs).
- `pnpm lint` and `pnpm lint:ci` (full orchestrator including knip) pass locally with exit 0, no new rule violations.
- `npx eslint --print-config web/src/app/layout.tsx | jq '.rules'` confirms `react-hooks/rules-of-hooks` and `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` both still resolve to severity `[2]` (error).

## Lockfile diff anatomy
The `pnpm-lock.yaml` diff is `+11 / -8`. Two distinct kinds of change:
1. **Intentional**: 3 new lines under `importers.web.devDependencies.eslint-plugin-react-hooks` recording the new direct-dep edge.
2. **Incidental noise** (4 hunks): pnpm 10.x peer-id cleanup. Identifiers like `eslint-plugin-import@2.32.0(@typescript-eslint/parser@8.57.1(...))(eslint@9.39.4(...))` collapse to `eslint-plugin-import@2.32.0` once pnpm proves the peer chain is unambiguous across all reference points. **No package version changes** — verified `next@15.5.15` and `eslint-config-next@15.5.15` are unchanged from `origin/main`.

If reviewers find the second category distracting we can split into a "lockfile-cleanup-only" prep PR, but it is fully equivalent semantics so consolidating is preferable.

## Out of scope
- `react-hooks/react-compiler` rule activation — needs a separate decision tied to React 19 / Next 15 compiler rollout strategy.
- Switching `eslint.config.mjs` from `FlatCompat.extends('next/core-web-vitals')` to direct `import reactHooks from 'eslint-plugin-react-hooks'` + explicit `plugins: { 'react-hooks': reactHooks }` registration. Would risk duplicate-plugin-name errors against `next/core-web-vitals` and is unnecessary for the pinning goal. (Doing it would also remove the need for the new knip exemption — that's a meaningful trade-off worth deferring rather than rushing.)

## Test plan
- [ ] `pnpm --filter web lint` passes locally and in CI (`code-quality / lint-and-test`).
- [ ] `pnpm --filter web lint:ci` passes (knip dep-health hard gate).
- [ ] `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` (CI default) succeeds with no integrity drift.
- [ ] `pnpm ls eslint-plugin-react-hooks --depth Infinity` shows a single 5.2.0 instance.

Closes the third of three sub-tasks in #1524.

chore(lint): promote exhaustive-deps to error in tests/** (PR 2 of #1524) (#1527)

## Summary
- Add `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps: 'error'` to the `tests/**` override
block in `web/eslint.config.mjs`. `next/core-web-vitals` defaults this
rule to `warn`, and `pnpm lint` uses `--quiet`, so warn-level
regressions are silently filtered out of CI — the same pitfall the
existing `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` comment above already
documents.
- Baseline scan via `npx eslint tests/ -f json` finds **zero** existing
`react-hooks/*` violations in `tests/**`, so this promotion is risk-free
and needs no grandfather list (unlike the legacy complexity overrides).
- Verified via `eslint --print-config` that `exhaustive-deps` resolves
to `[2]` (error) on a real `tests/unit/**.tsx` file. `pnpm lint` passes
locally.

This is **PR 2 of 3** from #1524. Independent of #1525 (PR 1) —
different block, no conflict; whichever merges first rebases trivially.

Out of scope (tracked in #1524):
- PR 3: pin `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` as a direct dev dep + evaluate
`react-hooks/react-compiler`.

## Test plan
- [ ] `pnpm --filter web lint` passes locally and in CI (`code-quality /
lint-and-test`).

author: Chris@ZooClaw
message:
chore(lint): explicit react-hooks rules + VSCode ESLint integration (PR 1 of #1524) (#1525)

## Summary
- Lock in `react-hooks/rules-of-hooks` as explicit `error` in the
`src/**` override (`web/eslint.config.mjs`) so a future
`eslint-config-next` preset change or override block can't silently drop
hooks lint coverage. `next/core-web-vitals` already enables it by
default; this re-declaration is contractual.
- Add `.vscode/extensions.json` recommending `dbaeumer.vscode-eslint` so
new contributors are prompted to install the official ESLint extension
on clone (React's [Editor Setup](https://react.dev/learn/editor-setup)
step 2).
- Add `.vscode/settings.json` with `eslint.workingDirectories` pointed
at `web/` (monorepo subdir requirement), `eslint.validate` for
js/jsx/ts/tsx, and `editor.codeActionsOnSave: { "source.fixAll.eslint":
"explicit" }` so saves give immediate red-squiggle / auto-fix feedback.
No `eslint.experimental.useFlatConfig` — vscode-eslint v3.x supports
flat config natively.

This is **PR 1 of 3** from #1524. Verified via `eslint --print-config`
that both `react-hooks/rules-of-hooks` and `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps`
resolve to `error` (severity `[2]`) on a real `src/` file. `pnpm lint`
passes with no new violations (rules were already error by default —
this PR makes the contract explicit).

Out of scope (tracked in #1524):
- PR 2: bump `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` to error in `tests/**`
(currently inherits `warn` from `next/core-web-vitals`, silenced by
`pnpm lint --quiet`).
- PR 3: pin `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` as a direct dev dep + evaluate
`react-hooks/react-compiler`.

## Test plan
- [ ] `pnpm --filter web lint` passes locally and in CI (`code-quality /
lint-and-test`).
- [ ] Open `web/` in VSCode with the recommended ESLint extension
installed; confirm `useEffect` missing-deps warnings appear inline on
save in a `src/**/*.tsx` file.
- [ ] `npx eslint --print-config web/src/app/layout.tsx | jq
'.rules["react-hooks/rules-of-hooks"]'` returns `[2]`.

Refs: #1524
